undergoing high-dose-rate (HDR) (192)Ir interstitial breast brachytherapy, and 
to desi
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 32206913. 2020/04/01 00:00. PURPOSE: To assess the radiation dose to the fetus of a pregnant patient 
undergoing high-dose-rate (HDR) (192)Ir interstitial breast brachytherapy, and 
to design a new patient setup and lead shielding technique that minimizes the 
fetal dose.
METHODS: Radiochromic films were placed between the slices of an anthropomorphic 
phantom modeling the patient. The pregnant woman was seated in a chair with the 
breast over a table and inside a leaded box. Dose var
